title: Relay: At-least-once delivery with client ack
description:
Current delivery is at-most-once: relay drain()s the queue (deletes rows), then delivers. If the WebSocket write fails mid-drain, the messages are gone.

Required:
- Add delivered_at column to message_queue (nullable)
- drain() fetches but does NOT delete — marks delivered_at = now()
- Client sends DeliverAck{message_id} after processing each Deliver
- Relay deletes row on DeliverAck receipt
- Redelivery: on reconnect, redeliver rows where delivered_at IS NULL OR delivered_at < (now - 1h)
- Client must deduplicate by envelope id (Uuid) to handle redelivery
- purge_expired() also purges delivered rows after 24h
close_reason: at-least-once queue delivery references drain()/DeliverAck architecture that was removed in broadcast redesign. Broadcast model delivers only to live connections; at-least-once is a future concern if reconnect+replay is added.
